import { StrictMode } from 'react'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'

import App from './App'
import * as serviceWorker from './serviceWorker'

const container = document.querySelector('#root') as HTMLElement
const root = createRoot(container)

root.render(
  <StrictMode>
    <App />
  </StrictMode>
)

// If you want your app to work offline and load faster, you can change
// unregister() to register() below. Note this comes with some pitfalls.
// Learn more about service workers: https://bit.ly/CRA-PWA
serviceWorker.unregister()

---APP---
import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

---ROUTES---
import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'
import { Routes, Route, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'

import { CategoryListPage } from './pages/CategoryListPage'
import { CategoryDetailPage } from './pages/CategoryDetailPage'
import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'
import { HomePage } from './pages/HomePage'
import { CheckoutPage } from './pages/CheckoutPage'
import { SuccessPage } from './pages/SuccessPage'

export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
  const location = useLocation()
  // Scroll to top when a path changes
  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
  }, [location.pathname])

  return (
    <>
      <div id="modal" />
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
        {children}
      </Routes>
    </>
  )
}

transcript.text: The runtime is fairly clear now: `App` wraps `BrowserRouter` -> Redux `Provider` -> `ThemeProvider`, injects `GlobalStyle`, and theme comes from `use-dark-mode`, which means Storybook needs real `window` storage state rather than a fake theme wrapper. I’m reading the route pages and shared components next so the stories copy actual JSX patterns and cover the async + portal cases the app really uses.

transcript.text: The app doesn’t have custom providers beyond router/store/theme, but it does have stateful runtime behavior that Storybook has to honor: `localStorage.darkMode`, `navigator.userAgent` checks for mobile carousels, Redux mutations during plays, and `#modal` portal rendering. I’m switching from analysis to implementation now: first the shared preview and MSW, then I’ll add stories incrementally and keep each one green with the Storybook Vitest project before moving to the next.

transcript.text: Checkout is passing, including the real route change to `/success`. I’m making one tiny cleanup in the shared router decorator now so unmatched navigations fall through quietly instead of logging warnings during later page-story runs.

I’ve covered the most stateful flow already, so the success page is mostly about verifying the second Redux slice and the basic page-shell variant. I’m adding both the filled and empty order cases so we don’t only validate the happy-path store payload.

transcript.text: TypeScript surfaced one Storybook-specific typing quirk rather than a runtime bug: for components with required props, this Storybook build expects default `args` on the meta even when the stories provide custom `render` functions. I’m patching those defaults now and then I’ll rerun the typecheck to make sure the preview and all stories are clean.

transcript.text:
Storybook now mirrors the real app runtime without touching app code. The shared setup in [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/preview.tsx), [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/main.ts), [preview-body.html](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/preview-body.html), and [msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) now provides the real provider order, `use-dark-mode` theme behavior, `GlobalStyle`, fresh Redux state per story, route-param support, the `#modal` portal root, and global MSW handlers for the restaurant API. I also added `msw-storybook-addon` in [package.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/package.json) and stabilized browser-mode dependency prebundling in [vite.config.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/vite.config.ts).

I added 10 colocated story files with real usage patterns and play assertions across shared components and pages: [Category.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/Category/Category.stories.tsx), [Header.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/Header/Header.stories.tsx), [Modal.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/Modal/Modal.stories.tsx), [RestaurantCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.stories.tsx), [HomePage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.stories.tsx), [CategoryListPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.stories.tsx), [CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx), [RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx), [CheckoutPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.stories.tsx), and [SuccessPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.stories.tsx).

Verification passed:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `npx vitest --project storybook ...` across the 10 new story files: 26/26 tests passed

One unrelated untracked file is still in the worktree: [prompt.md](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md).

items.text: Inspect app entrypoints, providers, CSS, routing, data fetching, browser-state reads, and portal usage
Set up Storybook preview, global mocks, portal roots, and any missing Storybook/MSW config

content.text: I’m going to trace the real app runtime first so the Storybook setup matches how the app actually boots. After that I’ll build the shared preview environment, then add stories one by one and run the Storybook Vitest project after each file until it passes.

import { StrictMode } from 'react'
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client'

import App from './App'
import * as serviceWorker from './serviceWorker'

const container = document.querySelector('#root') as HTMLElement
const root = createRoot(container)

root.render(
  <StrictMode>
    <App />
  </StrictMode>
)

// If you want your app to work offline and load faster, you can change
// unregister() to register() below. Note this comes with some pitfalls.
// Learn more about service workers: https://bit.ly/CRA-PWA
serviceWorker.unregister()

---APP---
import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

---ROUTES---
import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'
import { Routes, Route, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'

import { CategoryListPage } from './pages/CategoryListPage'
import { CategoryDetailPage } from './pages/CategoryDetailPage'
import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'
import { HomePage } from './pages/HomePage'
import { CheckoutPage } from './pages/CheckoutPage'
import { SuccessPage } from './pages/SuccessPage'

export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
  const location = useLocation()
  // Scroll to top when a path changes
  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
  }, [location.pathname])

  return (
    <>
      <div id="modal" />
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
        {children}
      </Routes>
    </>
  )
}

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/app-state/store.ts && printf '\\n---API INDEX---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' src/api/index.ts && printf '\\n---API HOOKS---\\n' && sed -n '1,260p' src/api/hooks.ts"

content.text: The runtime is fairly clear now: `App` wraps `BrowserRouter` -> Redux `Provider` -> `ThemeProvider`, injects `GlobalStyle`, and theme comes from `use-dark-mode`, which means Storybook needs real `window` storage state rather than a fake theme wrapper. I’m reading the route pages and shared components next so the stories copy actual JSX patterns and cover the async + portal cases the app really uses.

I’m checking the remaining form and helper components now so the stories use the same interaction flows as the real pages instead of custom harnesses.

content.text: The app doesn’t have custom providers beyond router/store/theme, but it does have stateful runtime behavior that Storybook has to honor: `localStorage.darkMode`, `navigator.userAgent` checks for mobile carousels, Redux mutations during plays, and `#modal` portal rendering. I’m switching from analysis to implementation now: first the shared preview and MSW, then I’ll add stories incrementally and keep each one green with the Storybook Vitest project before moving to the next.

Once that’s in place I’ll start with a simple reusable component story to validate the baseline.

content.text: The remaining big interaction surface is checkout, because it’s the one place the app mutates both Redux slices and routes away. I’m adding that now with the route constrained to `/checkout`, so a successful play can prove the real navigation to `/success` happened instead of just leaving the same component mounted.

content.text: TypeScript surfaced one Storybook-specific typing quirk rather than a runtime bug: for components with required props, this Storybook build expects default `args` on the meta even when the stories provide custom `render` functions. I’m patching those defaults now and then I’ll rerun the typecheck to make sure the preview and all stories are clean.

content.text: I noticed `mockdate` ended up unused once the page walkthrough was complete, so I’m removing it rather than leaving an unnecessary dependency behind. The app doesn’t actually have render-time date logic, so keeping the dependency would just add noise.

content.text:
Storybook now mirrors the real app runtime without touching app code. The shared setup in [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/preview.tsx), [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/main.ts), [preview-body.html](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/preview-body.html), and [msw-handlers.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/msw-handlers.ts) now provides the real provider order, `use-dark-mode` theme behavior, `GlobalStyle`, fresh Redux state per story, route-param support, the `#modal` portal root, and global MSW handlers for the restaurant API. I also added `msw-storybook-addon` in [package.json](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/package.json) and stabilized browser-mode dependency prebundling in [vite.config.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/vite.config.ts).

I added 10 colocated story files with real usage patterns and play assertions across shared components and pages: [Category.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/Category/Category.stories.tsx), [Header.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/Header/Header.stories.tsx), [Modal.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/Modal/Modal.stories.tsx), [RestaurantCard.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/components/RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.stories.tsx), [HomePage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.stories.tsx), [CategoryListPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/CategoryListPage/CategoryListPage.stories.tsx), [CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx), [RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx), [CheckoutPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/CheckoutPage/CheckoutPage.stories.tsx), and [SuccessPage.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/src/pages/SuccessPage/SuccessPage.stories.tsx).

Verification passed:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `npx vitest --project storybook ...` across the 10 new story files: 26/26 tests passed

One unrelated untracked file is still in the worktree: [prompt.md](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-10T16-09-49Z-4f792e46/project/.storybook/eval-results/prompt.md).
